text: their immunity /c125 Growing children and adolescents require more calcium. Though recommended dietary allowances for calcium are about 600-800 mg/day, it is desirable to give higher quantities of calcium for adolescents to achieve high peak bone mass. To achieve optimal peak bone mass, it is recommended to consume calcium rich foods like milk and milk products, fox tail millet (Ragi), til etc. Young children below the age of 5 years should be given less bulky foods, rich in energy and protein such as legumes, pulses, nuts, edible oil/ghee, sugar, milk and eggs. Vegetables including green leafy vegetables and locally available seasonal fruits should be part of their daily menu. Snacks make a useful contribution to the nutrient requirements, particularly in older children and adolescents.
